steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank sits inside the phrase "ratified in ______" which interrupts the sentence between the subject "The 12th amendment" and the main verb "separated." An interrupting phrase must be set off with matching punctuation on both sides. The opening comma before "ratified" is already given, so the blank needs a closing comma after "1804" to complete the pair.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '1804,': Closes the interrupter with a comma, matching the opening comma. The sentence reads smoothly: "The 12th amendment, ratified in 1804, separated..." ✓
  • Choice B — '1804—': An em dash cannot pair with a comma. Mixing an opening comma with a closing em dash produces mismatched punctuation. ✗
  • Choice C — '1804': No closing punctuation leaves the interrupter unclosed, running "1804 separated" together with no boundary. ✗
  • Choice D — '1804:': A colon introduces a list or explanation and cannot close an interrupter that opened with a comma. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
